description: Bring type-expr resolution in-house. A compiler2-owned resolver turns @spec, @type, param-annotation, and extern token bodies into hard Ty: structure (list, union, tuple, arrow) via compiler2 own Types; names via namespace lookup -> TypeName -> read TypeDefined (wait/demand on miss); scalars and builtins (integer, utf8, pid) minted directly with inner. No ModuleTypeEnv assembled -- the namespace is the env, prelude types visible by structural sharing. Grammar inspired by crate::type_expr::parse_type_expr but resolving via facts, not a blob. Verify: resolver unit tests over the worked examples (foo(SomeModule.t(float),integer); a plain alias; a refines brand).
title: In-house type-expr resolver: name -> hard Ty via namespace + TypeDefined
